profile.name: Dr. Beatriz Villarroel
roles: Research Associate, Instituto de Astrofísica de Canarias (IAC)
profile.summary: Swedish-Spanish astrophysicist whose VASCO project identified thousands of anomalous transient flashes in pre-satellite-era photographic plates. Her peer-reviewed findings - including a 22-sigma statistical result suggesting reflective objects in high Earth orbit before satellites existed - represent among the most rigorously published astronomical data in UAP-adjacent science.
early_career: Published AGN research in Nature Physics (2014) establishing mainstream astrophysics credentials
Developed statistical methods for large catalog cross-matching at Uppsala and IAC

key_events.event: Completed PhD at Uppsala University on active galactic nuclei. Began developing the VASCO methodology at IAC.
Submitted VASCO Paper I (arXiv:1911.05068) documenting the Vanishing and Appearing Sources project methodology and first results comparing USNO-B1.0 against Pan-STARRS DR1.
VASCO Paper I published in The Astronomical Journal (159:8). Of 15,593+ manually reviewed plate pairs, 798 objects classified as genuinely vanished with no known explanation.
Launched VASCO Citizen Science Project, recruiting public volunteers for visual inspection of plate image pairs.
Awarded L'Oreal-UNESCO For Women in Science Prize (Sweden) for the VASCO project. Began analysis of short-duration transient events in the first Palomar Sky Survey plates.
Awarded L'Oreal-UNESCO International Rising Talents Prize for Europe. Expanded VASCO to use the 10.4-meter Gran Telescopio Canarias for deep follow-up imaging.

Presented VASCO and POSS transient findings at the inaugural Sol Foundation Symposium, connecting with Garry Nolan and Avi Loeb's empirical UAP research programs. Joined the Sol Foundation Advisory Board.

Published MNRAS paper documenting a bright triple transient - three co-located point sources vanishing within 50 minutes - on a July 19, 1952 Palomar plate coinciding exactly with the Washington D.C. UFO flap.

Presented findings at the French 3AF Paris UAP conference alongside scientists and government officials. Participated in joint panel with Garry Nolan and Avi Loeb at the Sol Foundation 2024 Symposium.

Published three concurrent papers: Scientific Reports study linking POSS-I transients to nuclear weapons tests and UAP reports (with Stephen Bruehl, Vanderbilt); PASP paper on aligned multiple-transient events; and ExoProbe proof-of-concept in MNRAS demonstrating real-time anomalous transient detection using Earth's shadow as a satellite filter.
Co-authored 'The New Science of Unidentified Aerospace-Undersea Phenomena (UAP)' (arXiv:2502.06794), a collaborative framework paper with researchers from multiple institutions.

associated_people.name: Dr. Garry Nolan
associated_people.relationship: Joint Sol Foundation Advisory Board members; shared panel at Sol Foundation 2024 Symposium. Nolan has publicly described Villarroel's POSS transient findings as landmark evidence for pre-satellite anomalous objects.

associated_people.name: Prof. Avi Loeb
associated_people.role: Harvard astrophysicist and Galileo Project director
associated_people.relationship: Sol Foundation colleagues and joint panel participants at the 2024 Symposium. Both pursue empirical technosignature searches - Loeb via ocean-floor spherules, Villarroel via archival photographic plates and real-time observatory networks.

associated_people.name: Dr. Jacques Vallee
associated_people.relationship: Co-appear on the 2025 'New Science of Unidentified Aerospace-Undersea Phenomena' arXiv paper. Both participate in Sol Foundation symposia. Villarroel has publicly endorsed Vallee's energy-analysis approach to UAP evidence.

disclosures.title: VASCO Paper I: USNO Objects Missing in Modern Sky Surveys
disclosures.notes: Foundational paper establishing the VASCO methodology and presenting 798 genuinely vanished objects from 15,593+ reviewed plate pairs. arXiv:1911.05068.

disclosures.title: A bright triple transient that vanished within 50 min (MNRAS)
disclosures.outlet: Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society (527:3)
disclosures.notes: Documents a three-point-source transient on a July 19, 1952 Palomar plate coinciding with the Washington D.C. UFO flap. The 22-sigma shadow-avoidance result presented here implies reflective objects in high Earth orbit before Sputnik.

disclosures.title: Transients in the POSS-I may be associated with nuclear testing and UAP reports
disclosures.notes: Statistical analysis with Stephen Bruehl (Vanderbilt) finding POSS-I transient events 45% more likely within one day of above-ground nuclear weapons tests and correlating weakly with historical UAP report volumes.

disclosures.title: Aligned, Multiple-transient Events in the First Palomar Sky Survey
disclosures.outlet: Publications of the Astronomical Society of the Pacific
disclosures.notes: Documents spatially aligned multi-point transient events across POSS-I plates with no conventional astronomical explanation.

disclosures.title: ExoProbe: A Cost-Effective Search for Extraterrestrial Probes in the Solar System
disclosures.outlet: Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society (546:2)
disclosures.notes: Proof-of-concept for a real-time multi-telescope detection network using Earth's shadow to filter known satellites. Analyzed 200,000+ Zwicky Transient Facility images.

disclosures.title: The New Science of Unidentified Aerospace-Undersea Phenomena (UAP)
disclosures.notes: Collaborative framework paper with researchers from multiple institutions including Jacques Vallee and others, outlining empirical approaches to UAP science.

disclosures.title: Sol Foundation 2024 Symposium - VASCO and POSS Findings
disclosures.notes: Joint panel with Garry Nolan and Avi Loeb presenting VASCO findings and the implications of pre-satellite photographic plate transients.

disclosures.title: 3AF Paris UAP Conference - Archival Astronomical Evidence
disclosures.outlet: 3AF (Association Aeronautique et Astronautique de France), Paris
disclosures.notes: Presented POSS transient findings to an audience of scientists and government officials at the French aerospace association's UAP science conference.

Aligned Multiple-transient Events in POSS - PASP (2025)
L'Oreal-UNESCO For Women in Science Prize - IAC Announcement (2021)
Did Astronomers Photograph UFOs Orbiting Earth in the 1950s? - Scientific American
sources.notes: Overview of POSS findings and skeptical perspectives

claims.claim: A 22-sigma statistical result shows that approximately one-third of 106,000+ identified photographic plate transients avoid Earth's shadow, implying they are solar reflections from highly reflective objects in high Earth orbit before the Sputnik era.

POSS-I transient events are 45% more likely to occur within one day of above-ground nuclear weapons tests, suggesting a correlation between nuclear detonations and anomalous aerial phenomena over the United States in the 1950s.

A bright triple transient - three co-located point sources vanishing within 50 minutes - appears on a July 19, 1952 Palomar plate, the same night as the Washington D.C. UFO flap documented by civilian and military radar.
Of 15,593+ manually reviewed Palomar plate pairs, 798 objects have been classified as genuinely vanished with no known natural or instrumental explanation.

career_connections.relationship: Joint Sol Foundation Advisory Board members; Nolan publicly praised VASCO findings.
Sol Foundation colleagues pursuing parallel empirical technosignature programs.
Co-authors on 2025 New Science of UAP framework paper; shared Sol Foundation symposia.
Principal investigator of the Vanishing and Appearing Sources Century of Observations project.
